plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-506 RP16.P07.M04.S13-RP16.P07.M06.S08 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-506 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP16.P07 workflow permission slice: the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice tail (the ten remaining rows), the full M05 Permission And Audit Binding cycle (twenty-two rows), and the M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head (the first eight rows) as three descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H16), Claude review packet (C16), and CP00-507 handoff, without opening governance, DLP, legal hold, or retention runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-506 is a clean, descriptor-only governance-core slice (RP16.P07.M04.S13-RP16.P07.M06.S08, risk B, 40 units) whose production_ready claim is honestly scoped and fully supported. Pack metadata (pack_id, risk_class B, unit_count 40, range, upstream CP00-505 / next CP00-507 / next_subphase RP16.P07.M06.S09) is consistent across manifest, review-baseline, registry binding (registry.js:443), contract projection (governance-core-contract.json:16) and the closeout plan (closeout-pack-plan.json:408). The deliverable distribution (failure_recovery 20, implementation 4, hermes_evidence 4, fixture 2, test 4, security_audit 4, claude_review 2 = 40) and micro-title distribution (Secondary Workflow Slice 10, Permission And Audit Binding 22, Synthetic Fixture Set 8) reconcile by hand against the 40 included units and are enforced by validateGovernanceCoreCp506Coverage (validators.js:4765). Descriptor-only and no-real-data boundaries hold: every row is descriptor_only:true / runtime_execution:false, freezeCp506Result (service.js:3289) and the CP506 no-write attestation set all dispatch/write/runtime/audit/database/object-storage/fixture-load flags to false, and ROW_EXTRAS carry only synthetic metadata (real_client_data_loaded:false, deny_over_allow_enforced:true, cross_tenant_access_allowed:false). The authority boundary is correctly encoded and validator-enforced: claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true; the Claude review packet is read_only:true with tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob. Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic review packet/receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false; H16 evidence packet is a descriptor-validation result, not product runtime). production_ready:false / runtime_ready:false / implementation_layer:descriptor present no over-claim and the production_ready gate is not weakened. Tests (model.test.js) and the contract validator (validate-rp16-governance-core-contract.mjs) assert coverage validity, descriptor-only rows, authority boundaries, exports, and contract deepEqual; command-evidence records the full validation suite plus npm test/validate passing with no real data and no product-state writes. Review-hardening preconditions are satisfied: auth preflight passed with non-empty valid JSON, prompt 2269B within limit, read-tools-used source inspection (not summary-only), no forbidden tools, and no recorded invalid review attempts. The post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are referenced but not yet on disk, which is expected — they are generated after this review per the hardening sequence — and the unrelated dirty progress-control-room UI files are correctly excluded from pack scope. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings. Note that this PASS is an independent read-only reviewer signal only; human final approval remains required for any enterprise-trust or runtime-opening claim, which the pack itself correctly enforces.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-506 governance core p07 workflow permission slice descriptor pack
